name: SoundCloud_Music_Discovery
description: Explores and discovers music on SoundCloud as the Mind's music curation and research layer. Searches tracks by query and genre, retrieves artist profiles, lists user catalogs, and browses playlists. Bridges the Mind's music research interests to actionable discovery in SoundCloud's ecosystem. READ-ONLY operations only.
progressive_unlock: Track and artist searching available immediately upon authentication. User profile and track catalog retrieval unlocks after first successful track search. Playlist exploration available once user profiles have been explored.
constraints: Never expose SoundCloud OAuth 2.0 Bearer token in logs or outputs
Respect SoundCloud rate limit of approximately 15,000 requests per day
Read-only operations only — no track uploads, playlist modifications, or user account changes permitted
Duration values are returned in milliseconds — convert to seconds or minutes for user display
Artwork URLs support size variants (replace -large with -t500x500 for different sizes). Always use appropriate sizing for context.
Stream URLs require valid OAuth token or client_id for playback — store URLs in STREAM.entity for session reuse
Do not cache search results beyond current session — implement fresh searches for each discovery session
Always validate track_id, user_id, and playlist_id formats before API calls (SoundCloud uses numeric IDs)
Handle rate limiting gracefully: implement exponential backoff on rate limit responses (429, 502)
Genre filtering is case-sensitive on some API versions — normalize genre input to lowercase

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed. Verify query strings are non-empty and IDs are valid numeric/string format.
Retrieve SoundCloud OAuth 2.0 Bearer token from TENET.apiKey.soundcloud. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'. Verify token validity before proceeding.
Determine intent: track discovery (search), track details (get metadata), artist exploration (user profile), catalog review (user tracks), or playlist curation (playlist details).

For track discovery: use SoundCloud_SearchTracks with query string. Optional: apply genre filter(s) to narrow results. Use limit and offset for pagination control. Store discovered track IDs in STREAM.entity for downstream operations.

For track metadata retrieval: use SoundCloud_GetTrack with track_id. Extract title, duration (convert milliseconds to minutes:seconds), playback_count, likes_count, genre, artwork_url, and stream_url. Store metadata in STREAM.entity for reference.

For artist exploration: use SoundCloud_GetUser with user_id extracted from track metadata or search results. Retrieve username, followers_count, track_count, and avatar_url. Store user profile data in STREAM.entity.

For catalog review: use SoundCloud_GetUserTracks with user_id. Apply pagination (limit and offset) to manage large catalogs. Retrieve all tracks by the artist, storing track IDs and titles in STREAM.entity for batch analysis.

For playlist exploration: use SoundCloud_GetPlaylists with playlist_id. Extract playlist metadata (title, description, track_count, creator info) and complete track listing. Store playlist composition in STREAM.entity for curation analysis.

Process duration values: all durations are in milliseconds. Convert to MM:SS format for user display. Example: 180000ms = 3:00 minutes. Store both raw millisecond values and human-readable format in STREAM.
Optimize artwork URLs for context-appropriate display. Replace -large with -t500x500 for smaller displays, -t300x300 for thumbnails. Store multiple variants in STREAM.entity for flexible rendering.

Compile discovery summary including total results found, top-ranked tracks/artists by engagement metrics (playback count, likes), genre distribution, and artist statistics. Push daily discovery summary to LTM with key: 'soundcloud_discovery_{date}'. Update STREAM with active search context and explored entities.
